name: Natural Gas / LNG
summary: Henry Hub nat gas -4.7% to $2.95 on Monday — US production strong, partially offsetting Gulf disruption. TTF pulled back slightly to €54.18/MWh (-1.9%). European storage still critical at 30%. Goldman base case TTF €72/MWh in Q2. Qatar Ras Laffan 17% capacity destroyed (5-year repair). Emergency energy measures in 4 European countries.

price_snapshots.delta_context: European gas was at moderate levels
Surged 45% in single day on Ras Laffan shutdown
Further spike after Iranian missiles caused 5-year damage

signals.content: QatarEnergy halted LNG production at Ras Laffan (world's largest LNG facility, ~20% of global supply) after attacks. European TTF surged 45%.
Iranian missiles damaged LNG Trains 4 & 6 at Ras Laffan. 17% of Qatar LNG capacity destroyed. Estimated $20B annual revenue loss. 5-year repair timeline. This is STRUCTURAL, not temporary.

European TTF surged 70% in March to €54.52/MWh — worst monthly gain since Sep 2021. Driven by Qatar Ras Laffan destruction (17% capacity, 5-year repair) + Hormuz blocking LNG tankers. European storage at 30% (historically low after harsh winter). Emergency measures in 4 European countries: Spain cut VAT from 21% to 10% on energy, Italy allocated €100M support, Slovakia and Slovenia introduced pump rationing. ECB froze rate cuts March 19 and raised inflation forecast. Atlantic Council: crisis 'could trigger European energy crisis' at 2022 levels. If blockade persists through summer refill season (May-Oct), €80-100/MWh realistic — industrial shutdowns begin at that level.

Henry Hub nat gas -4.7% to $2.95 on Monday. US production strong, partially offsetting Gulf disruption. TTF pulled back slightly to €54.18 (-1.9%). European storage still critical at 30%. Goldman base case TTF €72/MWh in Q2. (Sources: yfinance, OilPriceAPI)

edges.context: Qatar was ~20% of global LNG. 17% capacity destroyed (5yr repair). Structural supply loss.
LNG shipments from Qatar affected by Hormuz closure. Even if production resumes, exports blocked.
India imports ~60% of LPG. LPG prices hiked. LNG supply disrupted by both Hormuz closure and Ras Laffan damage.
Natural gas is primary feedstock for nitrogen-based fertilizers (ammonia, urea). Gas price spike → fertilizer cost spike.
Oil and gas prices linked. LNG contracts often oil-indexed. Dual energy shock.

trigger_points.mechanism: Would trigger European industrial demand destruction and global fertilizer crisis. India heavily exposed through fertilizer and LPG imports.
